biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:WORSENING — 7/7 tool-using units (100%; units 4,6,8,21,25,32,34); ls-via-Bash head/tail split (unit 4), grep-rln-via-Bash and rg-l-via-Bash x2 and rg-via-Bash x3+ and cat|grep pipeline on package.json (unit 4), grep-n-via-Bash and rg-via-Bash as section anchors and wc-l-via-Bash (unit 6), rg-via-Bash x3+ as section anchors between Edit calls (unit 8), rg-l-via-Bash and for-loop-rg-via-Bash (unit 21), ls-via-Bash (unit 25), find-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x6+ (unit 32), cat-n-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x4+ and for-loop-Bash success=false (unit 34); rate 100% EXCEEDS prior cycle peak of 92%; zero internalization across 28+ consecutive measured cycles; all prior cycles flagged as STABLE but rate increase this cycle warrants WORSENING classification

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — units 6,8,32; unit 6: 7+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg-via-Bash section anchors and Reads; unit 8: Edit(success=false) then rg-via-Bash anchor then Read then 7+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg anchors; unit 32: 3 Edit calls on data-history-v3.tsx plus 6 Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx plus 3 Edit calls on s1-upload.tsx; consistent with all prior measured cycles; document-editing thrash on plan.md co-occurs with shell_tool_preference_violations as section-locating strategy in both units 6 and 8, confirming the causal link between the two patterns

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:IMPROVING — unit 4 only; Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false) before ls-via-Bash pivot to correct path; single instance this cycle vs. double-instance in the prior three consecutive cycles; first observed reduction from double to single instance; cross-session internalization still zero (wrong path still attempted) but frequency reduced; tentative improvement pending next cycle confirmation

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg-via-Bash anchor plus Read plus successful Edit; unit 34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before /usr/bin/grep anchor plus successful Edit; two instances consistent with all prior ten measured cycles at 1-2 instances per cycle; no improvement trend across 11+ consecutive cycles

rm_instead_of_trash:ABSENT — no Bash rm call observed in any unit this cycle; prior three consecutive cycles each contained one instance in unit 34; absence may reflect lighter filesystem work in this session rather than genuine correction; insufficient signal to reclassify; monitor next cycle
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations rate has escalated from 92% to 100% across tool-using units for the first time in measured history; the pattern is no longer STABLE — it is now structural and universal; the only novel recommendation given prior cycles have exhausted standard remediation suggestions is to investigate whether a PreToolUse hook can intercept Bash calls matching ls|grep|cat|find|rg|wc patterns when a dedicated tool (Glob, Grep, Read) would suffice, and emit a blocking warning; hook enforcement has proven effective for rm-vs-trash and could be applied here
path_assumption_bias shows first measurable improvement in 18+ cycles (double to single instance); no action recommended; continue monitoring to confirm trend before updating classification

re_edit_thrash and optimistic_edit_without_read remain unchanged across 10+ cycles; reclassifying both from monitoring priority to structural baseline; only noteworthy if rate changes significantly in either direction
